title: Structural sugar, alignment, platform branches, and folds
summary: Focused modules reduce repetitive structural syntax while folds are the only feature allowed to collapse vertical source rows.
details: Designated initializers drop the leading dot, tighten equals, and collapse .field = field or equivalent simple member access to a field pun; multiline aggregates align equals.
dans::logic::implies(P, Q) becomes (P) => (Q) through Tree-sitter argument boundaries, including nested calls.
Header trailing-return arrows and enum values align with inline padding based on rendered rather than raw widths.
Classic do/while(0) macro wrappers in headers are displayed as plain braced macro blocks without merging rows.
Platform-aware preprocessor evaluation grays and folds only branches whose known OS/compiler/attribute condition is decidable; unknown or compound conditions stay normal.
Contract blocks, consecutive static assertions, triple-slash documentation runs, and hpp outline nodes may fold; named non-detail namespaces stay open while detail/anonymous namespaces may fold.
Closed folds use a single gray summary row and are the sole authorized mechanism that changes the visible source-row mapping.

provenance.reference: lua/custom/dans_frontend_cpp/designated.lua, logic.lua, arrow_align.lua, enum_align.lua, macro_def.lua, ppif.lua, fold.lua
provenance.note: Each focused module owns one structural presentation category.

evidence.reference: tests/frontend_spec.lua designated/alignment/fold cases; tests/logic_spec.lua; tests/ppif_spec.lua; tests/fold_spec.lua
evidence.claim: Structural transforms, conservative preprocessor evaluation, and fold levels are covered independently.

questions: Which feature may collapse source rows?
How are unknown preprocessor conditions treated?
Why does header arrow alignment need a rendered-width model?

last_verified.method: Focused module/source audit and structural, logic, preprocessor, fold, interaction, and golden tests.
